content.text:
## Error Type
Console Error

## Error Message
Encountered a script tag while rendering React component. Scripts inside React components are never executed when rendering on the client. Consider using template tag instead (https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/template).


    at script (unknown:0:0)
    at RootLayout (app/layout.tsx:25:7)

## Code Frame
  23 |     <head>
  24 |       <JsonLdScripts />
> 25 |       <script
     |       ^
  26 |         dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
  27 |           __html: `
  28 |               try {

Next.js version: 16.2.4 (Turbopack)

content.thinking: Let me look at the file to understand the issue.

In Next.js 16, putting a `<script>` tag directly inside the `<head>` of a layout triggers this error. The recommended approach is to use `next/script` with `strategy="beforeInteractive"` or move the script. Actually, in Next.js 16, inline scripts in the head need to use the `template` tag or `next/script`.

The error message says: "Consider using template tag instead". But actually the proper fix in Next.js 16 is to use `next/script` with `beforeInteractive` strategy, or to put the script using the `Script` component.

Actually, let me think. The error is from React itself - "Encountered a script tag while rendering React component. Scripts inside React components are never executed when rendering on the client." This is a React 19 / Next 16 behavior.

The recommended approach: use `next/script` with `strategy="beforeInteractive"`. Let me check the Next.js 16 docs behavior.

Actually in Next.js 16, inline scripts in layout head should be handled. The proper way is to use the `Script` component from `next/script`. But `beforeInteractive` scripts must be placed in the root layout. Let me use that.

Let me rewrite using next/script

content.text: This is a Next.js 16 / React 19 behavior — inline `<script>` tags rendered directly in a component's JSX are no longer executed. The fix is to use Next's `Script` component with a `beforeInteractive` strategy.

edits.newText:
import { Script } from "next/script";
import type { Metadata } from "next";

<JsonLdScripts />
      <Script
        id="theme-color-script"
        strategy="beforeInteractive"
        dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
          __html: `
              try {
                if (localStorage.theme === 'dark' || ((!('theme' in localStorage) || localStorage.theme === 'system') && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches)) {
                  document.querySelector('meta[name="theme-color"]').setAttribute('content', '${META_THEME_COLORS.dark}')
                }
              } catch (_) {}
            `,
        }}
      />
edits.oldText:

                  document.querySelector('meta[name="theme-color"]').setAttribute('content', '${META_THEME_COLORS.dark}')
                }
              } catch (_) {}
            `,
        }}
      />

Let me give a concise summary

content.text:
Typecheck passes. Here's what I changed and why:

## Root cause
Next.js 16 / React 19 no longer executes (or even tolerates) bare `<script>` tags rendered directly in component JSX — React warns `Scripts inside React components are never executed when rendering on the client`. Your `RootLayout` head had one inline `<script>` for the theme-color bootstrap, and `JsonLdScripts` had several more for structured data.

## Fix
Switched both to Next's `Script` component with `strategy="beforeInteractive"`, which is the supported way to inject scripts into the initial HTML in Next 16:

- `app/layout.tsx` — wrapped the theme-color bootstrap in `<Script id="theme-color-script" strategy="beforeInteractive" ... />`.
- `seo/json-ld.tsx` — replaced the bare `<script>` in `JsonLdScript` with `<Script>` and added a unique `id` to each call (`json-ld-website`, `json-ld-software-source-code`, `json-ld-organization`, `json-ld-breadcrumb`), since `beforeInteractive`/`afterInteractive` require an `id`.

Notes:
- `next/script` is a **default export**, so the import is `import Script from "next/script"` (not a named import).
- `beforeInteractive` scripts must live in the root layout, which both files satisfy (JSON-LD is rendered in `RootLayout`'s head).
- JSON-LD with `application/ld+json` stays in the initial HTML, so SEO crawlers still see it.

`pnpm typecheck` now passes with no errors.
